source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
title: Ensure Node Auto-Repair is enabled for GKE nodes

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
input_row.title: Ensure Node Auto-Repair is enabled for GKE nodes
input_row.description: Nodes in a degraded state are an unknown quantity and so may pose a security risk.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes Engine's node auto-repair feature helps you keep the nodes in the cluster in a healthy, running state. When enabled, Kubernetes Engine makes periodic checks on the health state of each node in the cluster. If a node fails consecutive health checks over an extended time period, Kubernetes Engine initiates a repair process for that node. Impact: If multiple nodes require repair, Kubernetes Engine might repair them in parallel. Kubernetes Engine limits number of repairs depending on the size of the cluster (bigger clusters have a higher limit) and the number of broken nodes in the cluster (limit decreases if many nodes are broken). Node auto-repair is not available on Alpha Clusters.
input_row.audit: Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list 2. From the list of clusters, select the desired cluster. For each Node pool, view the Node pool Details pane and ensure that under the 'Management' heading, 'Auto- repair' is set to 'Enabled'. Using Command Line: To check the existence of node auto-repair for an existing cluster's node pool, run: gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <compute_zone> --format json | jq '.management' Ensure the output of the above command has JSON key attribute autoRepair set to true: { "autoRepair": true }
input_row.remediation: Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list 2. Select the Kubernetes cluster containing the node pool for which auto-repair is disabled. 3. Select the Node pool by clicking on the name of the pool. 4. Navigate to the Node pool details pane and click EDIT. 5. Under the Management heading, check the Enable auto-repair box. 6. Click SAVE. 7. Repeat steps 2-6 for every cluster and node pool with auto-upgrade disabled. Using Command Line To enable node auto-repair for an existing cluster's Node pool: gcloud container node-pools update <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <compute_zone> --enable-autorepair Default Value: Node auto-repair is enabled by default. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/node-auto-repair
input_row.impact: If multiple nodes require repair, Kubernetes Engine might repair them in parallel. Kubernetes Engine limits number of repairs depending on the size of the cluster (bigger clusters have a higher limit) and the number of broken nodes in the cluster (limit decreases if many nodes are broken). Node auto-repair is not available on Alpha Clusters.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- **Automated:** The control can be verified using GCP APIs or the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud), as indicated by the presence of specific commands in the audit section of the control.
- **GCP APIs query this property:** Yes, the audit instructions provide a `gcloud` command that checks node pool management attributes, specifically the `autoRepair` setting.
- **GCP CLI command provided:** The audit section mentions a `gcloud` command to describe the node pool and check the `autoRepair` attribute.
- **Standard GCP service property:** Yes, this concerns a standard GKE cluster property related to node management.
- **No need for SSH/RDP or human judgement:** The attributes can be checked programmatically.
- **Documentation references:** The control references official GKE documentation on node auto-repair, supporting the use of APIs or CLI commands for verification.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_nodepool_auto_repair_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would perform the following checks:

- **Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)** to verify the node auto-repair setting for each node pool in a GKE cluster.
- **Command to use:** `gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <compute_zone> --format json | jq '.management'`
- **Configuration check:** Ensure the JSON key attribute `autoRepair` is set to `true`.
- **Specific resources to enumerate:** All GKE node pools across all clusters within the organization or monitored projects.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**

  - **Pass:** The `autoRepair` attribute is `true`, indicating that auto-repair is enabled for the node pool.
  - **Fail:** The `autoRepair` attribute is `false` or not set, indicating that auto-repair is not enabled.

This approach ensures that each node pool in the GKE clusters is configured to automatically handle node repair, maintaining the desired security and operational state.
